I felt really good about myself. People see me and they say, "You know what? You look like a little Craig Monson." I said, "A little? I ain't no little [ __ ] body. I don't know what exercise is like squats, deadlifts, bench press, and pull-ups. Now, because he was in a gang, a lot of his training wasn't done in fancy gyms. He trained wherever he could, sometimes in backyards, garages, and even in prison gyms when he was locked up. Yep, Craig Monson spent some time behind bars, and even then, he didn't stop lifting. was head to toe front to back there's some flaws in his armor that I exploited and that's the whole key at the Mr. Olympia is that I wanted to bring the attention away from the champion's strong point and his strengths and exploit his weaknesses and I think I did a pretty good job last year. I got about 5 lb more of muscle and uh I'm still riding high convinced that I did the best I could do last year. Um no, maybe last year my best wasn't good enough in the eyes of the few judges that judged that contest. But after I went on tour and around the world and around the country, I came to realize that my best was more than good enough to to win that show. Uh maybe with a different panel of judges. So the bittersweet taste of take taking second place in the Mr. Olympia was slowly um turned into a positive reinforcement that I did everything right. I did everything I could do. People were pleased with the product that I put up there and the main thing was was that I was pleased because there was nothing I would have changed and there's nothing that I thought I should have changed with the package I put on on stage last year. So last year being last year and this year being this year injury free more time to prepare, 5 lb heavier uh equally motivated and seeing the light at the end of the tunnel and recognizing the weaknesses in the champion's armor, I'm ready to exploit it to its fullest and uh in 1 week I'll find out what the champion is really made of and hopefully uh he'll come to the stage with his best product because it's been 11 years. Actually, it's been 12 years since Mr. Olympia was defeated on stage. The year I graduated high school in 1984 was the year that Lee Haney won his first Mr. Olympia beating Samir Bannout. It hasn't been done since and uh I always knew that somehow before my career was over that if I was ever going to win the Mr. Olympia, I was going to take the title from the reigning champion. I didn't want to inherit it by retirement. I didn't want to win it by default meaning the champion gets injured and couldn't compete. I wanted to go to the Mr. Olympia and I saw myself doing this, going to the Mr. Olympia and taking the title from the champion and there would be no ifs, ands, buts about the competition.
